ud8.1:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
ud8.1:1.3: At that time the *Bhagavā* was instructing, encouraging, stirring up, and gladdening the *bhikkhus* with a talk on *dhamma* connected with *nibbāna*.
ud8.1:1.4: There the *bhikkhus* listen to the *dhamma*, taking it to heart, attending carefully, applying their whole mind, with ears ready.
ud8.1:2.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, understanding the significance of this, at that time uttered this inspired utterance:
ud8.1:3.1: “There is, *bhikkhus*, that dimension [*tadāyatanaṁ*], where there is neither earth, nor water, nor fire, nor air; neither the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], nor the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], nor the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], nor the dimension of neither-perception-nor-non-perception [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*]; neither this world nor another world, nor moon and sun.
ud8.1:3.2: There, I say, *bhikkhus*, there is no coming, no going, no staying, no passing away, no reappearing;
ud8.1:3.3: it is without footing, without occurrence, without object.
